If at the time of the Sun's entry into Scorpio Jupiter be in
Aquarius, the Moon in Taurus, and Mars and Saturn in Capricorn,
there would be excellent growth of corn, but later there would be
outbreak of diseases and threat of an enemy attack.

The Sun occupying Scorpio, being surrounded by malefics on
both sides, destroys crops. If there be a malefic in the 7th house,
crops would be destroyed even though they may grow well.

A malefic in the second house from Scorpio, being unaspectcd
by benefics, will destroy the crops sown first; but will enable those
sown subsequently to thrive well.
